diagrams.description: This timeline traces the evolution of Demography from early enumerations to modern population science. Key milestones: ancient censuses (Babylon c. 3800 BCE, Roman c. 6 CE); John Graunt’s 1662 life table analysis, founding demography; Edmund Halley’s 1693 life table; Thomas Malthus’s 1798 ‘Essay on the Principle of Population’; the first modern censuses (US 1790, UK 1801); Adolphe Quetelet’s 1835 statistical sociology; Alfred Lotka’s 1907 stable population theory; the demographic transition model (Notestein, 1945); the 1946 establishment of the UN Population Division; the 1954 World Population Conference; the 1974 Bucharest Conference; the 1994 Cairo Conference on Population and Development; global population milestones (1 billion 1804, 7 billion 2011, 8 billion 2022); and contemporary developments in big data and population projections.
